reviews.review_description: Would be great if it had normal control sorry I bought no point in cockpit view can't look around and no instruments I have played much better cardboard games. The people who gave good ratings must be easily entertained nothing special

developer_response.body: Hi Jesch, Please check the last update! More control options now!

reviews.review_description:
I just got an oculus Go, and this game is one that can really transport you to a world far away, in high-paced action - without making you sick!
The visuals are beautiful, and I can't wait to gain a bit more CP on the solo before going into the multi-player modes!
Game gets stale after awhile and nit really worth the 8 dollars
First game I got in the oculus go and I love it

reviews.review_title: Arcade Spaceship PvP MMO - Av Sum!
reviews.review_description:
What a belter! Just buckle up and jump in!  Then shoot the s*** out of other players in VR spacetime!   I mean, come on, if you're a gamer, what more could be awesome?!

There's are progression trees for different ships, tech, weaponry, attributes and other stats. I've not unlocked them all yet but they're enough to keep me interested as I progress. And how satisfying is it to launch a homing missile in space whilst barrel-rolling round incoming enemy fire whilst firing off your cannons at the other player in the reticule resulting in two simultaneous kill tags?! Ohhh, let me tell you, the satisfaction is immense! 

I was a fan of the original cathode-ray screen, Star Wars arcade game, from the late '70s, that you sat inside to play and then fly down the exhaust channel of the Death Star in an XWing... now that kinda thing is in VR and against real people. I love it! 

There are options for 1st and 3rd person view, the controls are good (I use the Mad Catz Razr). I don't have the issues that a few on here have here with the  control options but I do know that the devs are working on more options so that players can play more comfortably on the settee as well as a swivel chair. I've never felt nauseous with the controls. I only feel ill when I'm in a flight sim or roller-coaster but it's obvious from the reviews that others have different responses. 

The devs have been super quick to my enquiries, via email mostly. No complaints there at all, they're obviously engaged with feedback. I'm very much looking forward to future updates and an increasing player base.

There may be an amendment to this review as things progress or proper problems are found. In the meantime, I have to say that I waited until the Daily Deal before purchasing and, now that I've played it, I'm well happy overall.

This experience is extraordinary, specially with the cockpit view and the go controller. It has become my favorite game but there are two things i would change;  1 it would be really nice to be able to use the go controller in the menu(and being able to select multiple items to recycle at once) and 2 there should be an option to turn off the forceful 3rd person view when you are tailed(and not have limited controls). Nevertheless this is an amazing game keep up the great work guys, gals and others

Oof. So Endspace has got this on lockdown. Do *exactly* what they do with some more minor adjustments. No need for it to feel like I'm flying something with wings, wings create G's and lack of G's in real life is what creates motion sickness. I just want to go where I look, and for it to be fully smooth. No linear corrections, no sudden changes. I'd much rather fly something that smoothly goes where I look.

Use Endspace physics with a little more acceleration (this will simulate the responsive flight style you all are looking for) and it's a winner.

You guys are doing great!!! I love the customization, and absolutely this is gonna put you guys so far ahead here. Love it!

If for any reason you all want any help with resolving the motion sickness issues, I can absolutely help. Please contact me at midnight32enterprises@gmail.com. Free of charge, can promise full competency. Graduated with a degree in Neuroscience from Vanderbilt, have studied the vestibular system in depth along with physics and VR programming and I know how to make this the great game it wants to be.
Looks good, plays great, and doesn't give me motion sickness.
I hope you guys have more tournaments!!!  I missed the last one.  :(   This game is amazing!  So fun to play.

A great looking game that is hurting due to lack of direct control with gamepad. It looks like developer is working on update to allow full controller movement. I will return to
this game once the update has been released. Currently having to steer with your head is a pain.

developer_response.body: Hi, Please try out the latest update for more control options

reviews.review_description: Very nice flying graphics

It would play better for me if instead of the option to sharp turn left or right in comfortable mode using the left and right click on the oculus go controler it did a barrel roll on left and right click and showed the roll in tunnel vision instead of flashing gold. Doing a barrel roll by sharply tilting my head is hurting my neck �
I am a solo player and I love how smooth and reactive this game is. The imagery is so beautiful to look at that it makes me feel like I am in another world!! Great job!!👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍

I’ll talk about the cons first.
The first mission will have you believe that this is a space game but after the intros are done you’re dropped into a really low res poorly designed city to fly around. Your ship moves like a slug so it takes for ever to flow over to mission points. The missions aren’t really clear on where to go to next or what to do when you get there. You don’t have much of a hud to guide you around. 

Now for the pros.
The game in FPS mode to me was really well made. The movement’s are very responsive and it makes you feel like you’re in an actual space ship. The shop area looks really nice and the UI that you get while you’re in there looks really crisp.

Overall.
I enjoyed the game for what it was but the initial flying around the city to get to a mission was slow and painful even with the boosts.
It’s good but it needs better graphics and when your in cockpit view to actually let you see the barrel roll

Great game and fun to play with friends.   During some of the campaigns the buildings are so close together it makes it hard to navigate to chase your targets.  I wish there were more open sky campaigns to make it more fun to zoom around however. The PvP matches are cool , but there’s only an inside and outside mode which alternates between matches, and I think it would be better if there were more scenes to choose from or an option to select outside matches in the asteroids instead of inside the maze.  Definitely works best when you are playing using a swivel chair.   Still a solid 4 out of 5 but if they made more open sky flying modes and campaigns and have us more scenes for player vs player then it’d be even better.   One of the best games on the Go, and definitely a fun way to spend a few hours .
All the useful powerups require vast quantities of resources that can only be acquired for real world currency. Single player story mode is rather dull. Haven't tried the multiplayer.
It’s fun, I haven’t played better flying and shooting kind of game on Oculus Go so far, but some moments are not so good.
Am really dissapointed and Oculus won't accept refund...bummer
Swivel chair gaming is weird. Pretty good game otherwise. I would have liked cockpit view for vr.
